{"key_event":"Great Society and civil-rights mobilization","reasoning":"Federal capacity expanded through Great Society administration, civil-rights enforcement, grants, Medicare/Medicaid, and Vietnam-era military/executive mobilization, though urban unrest and legitimacy strains limited any move toward a perfect score. Civil society also strengthened markedly as civil-rights, antiwar, student, media, and court-backed rights mobilization expanded the ability of citizens—especially previously excluded Black citizens—to organize and constrain government.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":8.6,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":9.7}
